changeSet.author: openelis-bioanalytical

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.workflow_page_template WHERE workflow_category = 'BIOANALYTICAL'

changeSet.comment: Insert predefined bioanalytical workflow page templates

column.name: name
column.value: Sample Reception & Registration

column.name: description
column.value: Receive samples (biological or pharmaceutical), assign identifiers, register metadata, and link to source laboratory or client

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Register sample receipt: Assign or retain Sample ID, capture sample type (API, tablet, plasma, serum, etc.), note source (Medical Lab or external client), record requested tests, document storage condition on arrival, receipt date/time, and receiving personnel. Link to bioequivalence study if applicable.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Sample Reception & Registration - Batch verification and metadata capture of incoming samples

column.name: name
column.value: Test Assignment & Preparation

column.name: description
column.value: Allocate test responsibilities, select analytical methodology (HPLC, LC-MS/MS, dissolution, etc.), and document sample preparation steps

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Assign tests to analysts/pharmacists by test category. Select analytical method (HPLC UV-Vis, LC-MS/MS, dissolution apparatus, etc.) from dropdown. Specify instrument ID, calibration status. Document sample preparation methodology. Define QC requirements (calibration curve criteria, control samples). Set expected analysis date.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Test Assignment & Preparation - Methodology selection and analyst assignment

column.name: name
column.value: Analytical Test Execution

column.name: description
column.value: Execute analytical tests, capture raw instrument data, document QC results, and perform quality control checks

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Record execution date/time and analyst ID. Upload or link calibration curve details (r² value, slope, intercept, acceptance criteria). Document QC sample results (Low/Medium/High concentrations) with acceptance limits. Upload raw data files (chromatograms, spectra). Record calculated results with units. Document any deviations. Verify ALCOA+ compliance (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate, Complete).

column.name: default_content
column.value: Analytical Test Execution - Raw data capture and QC validation

column.name: name
column.value: Reporting & Release

column.name: description
column.value: Compile analytical results, validate against acceptance criteria, perform multi-level review, and release reports

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Compile all sample results. Validate against acceptance criteria. Perform Primary Review (analyst validation). Perform Secondary Review (senior scientist/QA). Obtain Final Approval (lab manager/study director). Select result distribution method (email, REDCap, LMIS integration, manual). Generate performance metrics dashboard. Document data integrity and audit trail.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Reporting & Release - Result compilation, validation, and report generation with embedded dashboard

column.name: name
column.value: Post-Test Storage & Archiving

column.name: description
column.value: Document sample storage hierarchy, retention periods, environmental monitoring, and data archiving

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Document quantity retained for storage. Determine transfer to biorepository (long-term storage at -80°C for biological samples, room temperature for pharmaceutical). Record storage location hierarchy (Room → Freezer → Shelf → Rack → Box → Position). Set retention period (2 years for bioequivalence, 5-10 years for regulatory, or custom). Set up environmental monitoring. Document raw data archiving locations. Record disposal criteria and methods. Maintain sample-result traceability.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Post-Test Storage & Archiving - Storage tracking, retention periods, and data archiving
